scenarios.title: Logging in to the campus website
scenarios.context: A student helper explains how to access a university website.

sentences.text: Open the school website first.
Click the student login button.
Use your student ID and password.
The system may ask you to verify your email.
After logging in, choose the course registration page.
If the page does not load, refresh your browser and try again.
Students who forget passwords should contact technical support before registration closes.

scenarios.title: Reserving a library study room
scenarios.context: A librarian explains how to reserve and use a group study room.

sentences.text: Choose a room on the library map.
Select the time your group needs.
You can reserve the room for two hours.
Bring your student card when you arrive.
Please cancel your booking if your plans change.
Rooms left empty for fifteen minutes may be given to others.
Food is not allowed, but covered drinks are fine in study rooms.

scenarios.title: Booking professor office hours
scenarios.context: A classmate explains how to book a meeting with a professor.

sentences.text: Check the office hours schedule online.
Pick a time slot before it fills up.
Write your question in the booking form.
Attach your draft so the professor can prepare.
Arrive five minutes early and wait outside politely.
If you cannot attend, cancel early so another student can take the slot.
Bring specific questions, because short meetings are easier when goals are clear.

scenarios.title: Preparing a group project meeting
scenarios.context: A team leader gives instructions before a project meeting.

sentences.text: Review last week's tasks before the meeting.
Upload your slides to the shared folder.
Each member should report progress in one minute.
Mention blockers early so we can solve them together.
After discussion, assign one clear owner to every task.
Record key decisions because memory is unreliable after long meetings.
Send the summary tonight so everyone starts tomorrow with the same priorities.

scenarios.title: Taking the bus to campus
scenarios.context: A student explains how to commute by bus in the morning.

sentences.text: Leave home ten minutes before the bus.
The number twelve bus stops near campus.
Use your student card for a cheaper fare.
Check the app in case the bus is delayed.
Get off at the library stop, not the stadium.
In rain, buses are crowded, so leave home even earlier.
If you miss one bus, the next usually arrives in fifteen minutes.

scenarios.title: Printing an assignment
scenarios.context: A teaching assistant explains how to print and submit homework.

sentences.text: Save your file as a PDF first.
Use double sided mode to save paper.
Add your name and student number on top.
Check page order before pressing the print button.
If the printer jams, ask the lab staff for help.
Staple the pages neatly and keep one photo as backup.
Submit before noon, because late work may lose points automatically.

scenarios.title: Checking in to a dorm
scenarios.context: A resident assistant explains dorm check in steps for new students.

sentences.text: Bring your passport and admission letter.
Sign the housing agreement at the front desk.
Collect your room key and laundry card.
Check furniture carefully and report any damage today.
Do not leave personal items in hallway common areas.
Quiet hours begin at ten, so use headphones at night.
If your key is lost, contact the front desk immediately to avoid penalties.

scenarios.title: Applying for a campus job
scenarios.context: A senior student shares steps for applying to campus jobs.

sentences.text: Search openings on the student employment portal.
Update your resume before submitting any application.
Write a short cover letter for each role.
Highlight class projects that match the job duties.
Prepare two references who can comment on your reliability.
During interviews, answer directly and give one specific example from experience.
After interviewing, send a thank you email because polite follow up can improve your chance.
